transcript.text: Yes, sorry, guys.
No worries.
All good.
Okay.
Also, I'm just adding note takers so that we can review back.
But, okay.
So, general point of the call, I just want to get on the same page, get all the details around how we're executing on RAMP.
So, my understanding is that the key difficulty here is that things come in big batches with the expectation that we'll turn them around quickly.
And in order to meet those, like, kind of scrunched timelines, that's why we're leveraging freelancers.
If things came at a normal cadence, could we have one CM, like you, Nika, support this account?
Or do we, yeah, I just want to get your thoughts on what are we doing currently?
What are the current challenges?
And what would we need to fix in order for one person to be able to support this account?
Yeah.
So, I'll jump ahead, Nika, if don't mind.
So, currently.
Basically, we have three people on the account.
So there's Nika, there's two freelancers.
We can technically run it with just Nika and one freelancer.

The problem is that if any one of them is sick or is on vacation or doesn't show up, which has happened with freelancers, sometimes the workload is a bit much, given the other accounts, because Nika is supporting two other accounts as well.
He's not on just Ramp.
So the thing with Kelvin really is that it's not, you know, I would say in the short term, it's not a huge deal if he moves off the account.
If he goes away or something, we can lean more on the other freelancer to take up, you know, his workload.
It's just that if she goes, if she's sick or something, or if she doesn't turn up one day, then we're going to be in a bit of a soup.
So it's more like a precautionary thing.
thing.
And that's my view.
Yeah, great.
Okay.
All right.

So is that accurate, though, that basically, okay, if the ramp requests came, if we could portion it out weekly to be the same amount every week, and we didn't have these last-minute requests, would that, what I'm asking, basically, is I think we are going to be asked at some point, we're going to, like, I'll definitely get pressure around us still using freelancers, and I think the goal will likely be how do we stop using freelancers?

So I just want to understand, is there a world in which we can ask ramp, hey, we need you to send these, or I just want to be able to communicate, like, to Andy, for instance, this is what we would need to ask of ramp, in order for it to be true, that we would just have one.
And CM in this account, or we would need to hire another CM to take on some of the work that Unique are doing for other accounts.
So anyway, is it true that us getting those last minute requests is the main issue here?
Or could we ask Ramp to operate a little bit differently so that just one person can support this account?
I don't think we can definitely ask them to send it to us in batches.
We can communicate that to Victoria, but I don't think that affects the resourcing because the bigger issue is we have five accounts, and Nika is on two other accounts.
So that's the overall kind of volume of work that we're handling right now.
And then you add, this is a separate discussion, but if you add the new data grid, SoW, into the mix, then it's pretty much impossible.
So that's my concern.
In terms of resources,
I'd say DataGrid is more critical than RAM right now, but for RAM specifically, we can ask them to space it out.
It reduces the risk of Kelvin not being on the account, but it does not mean that we can get rid of the other freelancer as well, if that makes sense.
And just to draw the picture, like weekly, we receive from 37 to 45 articles to produce.
So that's the average number of articles that is with us.
Okay.
So 37 to 45 is the average weekly output for RAMP right now?
Yeah, approximately.
Okay.
Okay.
I'm curious about what our SoW says, because I feel like that's higher than what our SoW is.
I don't think RAMP's SoW is anywhere close to...
Okay.
So...
Yeah, it's been changing a lot.
Yeah.
That is what we currently have in the client database, says 15 to 40 programmatic pages per week, four to five editorial.
So I guess that is actually in line with what we've agreed upon or we've been doing.
So, okay.
Interesting.
All right.
So I don't know why we would ever have just one CM on this account.
Let me chat with Andy about that.
Okay.
So essentially what I need to relay is there's some operational complexity here, which is, Nika, you're serving two accounts, which have like higher.
Four in total.
DataGrid, Rapid, and Rapid.
Four.
It's four.
All right.
Not two.
Okay.
So you're currently servicing four accounts in at least some capacity and, okay.
Okay.
And Ramp, some of the complexity is that we get requests to do these things on a tight turnaround.
They're an important logo for us.
We accommodate.
In order to be flexible, we have relied upon freelancers.
And then also there's just the question of bandwidth because you're across multiple accounts.
Okay.
And then even if we were to ask Ramp to portion it out a little bit more dependably, that's still a really high volume.
Are we doing, we're not currently doing true programmatic for Ramp or we are?
We're not if we're using freelancers, I guess.
Um, it's an interesting question.
Uh, I think we are doing programmatic, which is why we are using freelancers because the pages are quite easy to generate.
Yeah.
we're not even using Atlas.
Yeah.
That's what I'm asking.
I think like when they say like, that's the question.

Question that Andy and anyone else will have is when they ask about programmatic is, think, like, because that'll be the next talking point will be, we just need to use Atlas, truly programmatic, and then we can keep up with that volume and have fewer people on it is going to be there.
Yeah, so if, yeah, I'm fully anticipating that.
So even if engineering does build us pipelines to run those programmatic pages, we'll still need somebody to actually run those pipelines.
And I don't think Nika has the capacity to run 45 pages by himself in Atlas and review it.
Because what the freelancers are doing essentially is just, it's the equivalent of pushing a button in Atlas right now.
They're doing it in perplexity instead of Atlas.
So the work doesn't really, the work, the time spent doesn't change, whether on Atlas or on perplexity.
So either way, the equation.
I don't know whether it's relevant, but we are also generating images for overview articles that is not automated yet.
We had talks, but yeah, that's the current scenario.
Okay.
Okay.
All right.
I will have a conversation with Andy.
Yeah.
Okay.
I'll have a conversation with Andy.
I might drop it into the internal ramp channel just so that you're both able to weigh in as needed.
I'll try and give her like a very concise overview.

I will probably just give her the option to have the conversation just like one-on-one with me if it feels like it's easier to just keep it really streamlined that way, or if she wants me to drop it into the channel so we can all weigh in.
I think what's complicated about this is that we are splitting resources across accounts.
So I think it's hard for...
Or her probably to get a picture of everyone's capacity, because sometimes it's just like you'll have, I don't know.
Anyway, I'll let her know that this is kind of the current state.
Yeah.
And then DataGrid is a separate question or a separate conversation.
And I think it's a little bit easier to advocate for more resources there.
It might help to combine the two of them, because RAMP, I think, is not as critical.
Like, let's say Kelvin comes on a two-month contract, right?
And then let's say for, you know, he gets assigned to DataGrid.
He can work on DataGrid and RAMP at the same time.
So if one extra person comes on DataGrid, I think they can take both those accounts.
So it doesn't have to be like one for DataGrid, one for RAMP.
Yeah.
So we don't know the details of Kelvin's employment, right?
No, I checked with Andy, she does not have any other additional, like no plans necessarily to my knowledge of where he'll be placed, but he's joining like in an official capacity next week.
So ideally, he'll support DataGrid and we can also use him on RAMP, but I just need to confirm that that's possible.
And then I, most importantly, I just need to explain to Andy, who's probably getting a lot of pressure also on resourcing, why this makes sense for this account.
So that's all I needed.
Cool.
And then I do think we are probably going to get a little bit more pressure around taking everything that we can into Atlas.
So I think that will probably be a short-term priority around programmatic.
And so we should probably just like have that kind of.
to able to So
On the horizon, because we will have questions about it.
Okay, cool.
Mara, does it mean that we may need to get rid of another freelancer who's on this project?
Does that mean that we need to?
Is that your question?
Yeah.
Yes.

I think in the instance, not in like, I'm just saying this is probably going to be just knowing like all of the internal communication that there has been, that we've had around like using Atlas and moving away from freelancers.
I think there's a momentary understanding of like why we're doing it and like we're fine for right now to do it.
But I do think eventually the kind of the narrative that we'll hear probably is we've communicated this.
Why are you still doing it?
So I just want to get ahead of that as much as possible while not constraining our team too much in the short term.
But we should probably.
Probably start working towards that reality.
And it's not a today thing.
We can just focus on getting the support that we need for the short term.
But in terms of long term, yeah, we should probably anticipate we'll need to move away from using freelancers.
But yeah, I mean, if they don't want us to use freelancers, we need another CM.
Like, it's that simple, because we've always been, like, I think, over capacity somehow, because three CMs to five accounts.
But it's always been a bit of a challenge.
So especially when somebody goes on leave.
So yeah.
Totally, which makes sense.
I think it's just, yeah, anyway, it totally makes sense.
OK, thank you for huddling.
I appreciate it.
There are a lot of little, like, this and that for the full picture here.
So I think that that can be challenging to relay.
So thank you for taking the time.
I'm going to condense this into a message that I think will be helpful for Andy.
And maybe I will share it with you guys just to confirm that I've captured everything.
But yeah.
Okay, cool.
Thanks for your time.
I will confirm on resources.
Ideal end result of this is just that Kelvin supports our accounts.
So I'll keep it posted.
Okay.
Thank you.
Thanks, guys.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on RAMP account execution challenges and resource allocation strategies.](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=12.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [RAMP workload: 37-45 articles/week, requiring 3 people (Nika + 2 freelancers)](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
  - [Operational complexity: Nika manages 4 accounts total, limiting bandwidth](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)
  - [Resource pressure: Moving away from freelancers requires either Atlas automation or additional CM hire](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)
  - [Short-term solution: Leverage Kelvin (new hire) for both DataGrid and RAMP support](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)

## Topics

### Current RAMP Workflow and Challenges

  - [3 people on account: Nika + 2 freelancers](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)
  - [Workload: 37-45 articles/week (aligns with SoW of 15-40 programmatic, 4-5 editorial)](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=258.0)
  - [Key issues:](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=21.0)
      - [Large batches with tight turnarounds](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=21.0)
      - [Risk if freelancers unavailable (sick/vacation)](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=69.0)
      - [Nika supporting 4 accounts total (RAMP, DataGrid, Rapid, one other)](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)

### Potential Solutions and Constraints

  - [Asking RAMP to space out requests: Helps but doesn't solve resource allocation](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [Atlas automation: Still requires manual oversight, doesn't significantly reduce time](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Image generation for overview articles not yet automated](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=482.0)
  - [Moving away from freelancers requires either full Atlas automation or additional CM hire](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)

### Resource Allocation Strategies

  - [Short-term: Utilize Kelvin (new hire) for both DataGrid and RAMP](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [Long-term: Anticipate pressure to move away from freelancers](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)
  - [Consider combining DataGrid and RAMP resource requests for efficiency](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)

### Internal Communication Strategy

  - [Mara to condense meeting insights for Andy, explaining:](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=502.0)
      - [Current state of RAMP account](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=502.0)
      - [Justification for current resource allocation](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
      - [Potential for Kelvin to support both DataGrid and RAMP](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [Anticipate questions about Atlas utilization and moving away from freelancers](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=639.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Mara to draft concise overview for Andy, potentially sharing in internal RAMP channel](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=502.0)
  - [Confirm Kelvin's availability to support both DataGrid and RAMP](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=594.0)
  - [Begin planning for increased Atlas utilization for programmatic content](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=639.0)
  - [Anticipate long-term shift away from freelancers, prepare transition strategy](https://fathom.video/share/XeAjyhhMjRqq9pwn_yikhmb4THsx5Bvz?tab=summary&timestamp=665.0)

action_items.description: Discuss w/ Andy - RAMP resourcing (4 accts, freelancers, tight turnarounds, high volume). Explain need for current setup.
Confirm w/ Andy - Kelvin to support DataGrid + RAMP accts next week. Verify feasibility.
Condense mtg info into msg for Andy. Cover RAMP complexities, resource splitting across accts, freelancer use rationale.
